name: Short Movie
description: Template for 5-10 minute short films ideal for YouTube / Vimeo etc.

subsections.name: Idea Wizard
subsections.description: Unsure of your overall story idea? Use the Idea Wizard to have a back-and-forth discussion where it will suggest you various directions to where you could take your story.
subsections.chat_system_prompt: You are an expert short film story development partner. Help the user develop their short film idea through creative discussion. Focus on economy of storytelling — every moment must count in a 5-10 minute film. Suggest visual storytelling, clear character arcs, and impactful endings.

subsections.name: Import Project
subsections.description: Bring an existing screenplay to seed beats, scenes, and the screenplay editor. Paste or upload your screenplay, optionally include guidance, and we will align beats, scenes, and screenplay sequences for you.

fields.label: Guidance (Optional)
fields.placeholder: Any specific guidance for how to align beats and scenes...

subsections.name: Core
subsections.description: Establish the foundational elements that will drive your short film. Focus on clarity and visual storytelling potential. Each element should be concise and work together to create a story that plays effectively at the scope you choose.

cards.label: Logline
cards.placeholder: Premise you can recite in one breath.

cards.label: Desire
cards.placeholder: What does the protagonist want? The specific, visible goal driving the story.

cards.label: Plan
cards.placeholder: What's the protagonist's plan to get what they want?

cards.label: Antagonistic Force
cards.placeholder: Person, system, or internal flaw that actively blocks goal.

cards.label: External Stakes
cards.placeholder: Life-and-death can be emotional, social, or literal.

cards.label: Internal Stakes
cards.placeholder: What emotional or psychological consequence faces the protagonist.

cards.label: Key Visual Motif / Payoff
cards.placeholder: An image or prop that appears in beat 1 and transforms by beat 8 (great shorthand for arc).

cards.label: Unique Element
cards.placeholder: World rule or similar; something that makes this story unique.

subsections.name: Characters
subsections.description: Define your short film's characters. In a 5-10 minute film, every character must earn their screen time. Focus on distinct voices, clear motivations, and how characters reveal theme through action.

fields.label: Core Trait
fields.placeholder: The defining quality that drives their actions

fields.label: Fatal Flaw
fields.placeholder: The weakness that creates internal conflict

fields.label: Want vs Need
fields.placeholder: What they pursue vs what they actually need

fields.label: Dialogue Style
fields.placeholder: How they speak — rhythm, vocabulary, verbal habits

fields.label: Role & Purpose
fields.placeholder: What function they serve in the story

subsections.name: Story
subsections.description: Build your story arc using these 8 beats. Each beat should be visual and contribute to the short-film runtime you choose. Focus on economy of storytelling – every moment should advance plot, reveal character, or enhance theme. Remember: in a short film, you can suggest more than you show.

cards.label: Hook / Opening Image
cards.placeholder: Grab attention with visual contradiction or immediate stakes. ‘What’s the tone, genre, and central tension?’

cards.label: Status Quo
cards.placeholder: One clear image or exchange that defines the protagonist’s normal world & flaw/need. ‘Who am I following and what’s missing?’

cards.label: Inciting Incident
cards.placeholder: Event that yanks the protagonist off balance; promise of conflict. ‘Why must something change right now?’

cards.label: Point of No Return
cards.placeholder: Hero chooses (or is forced) to pursue a goal; tension escalates. ‘What concrete objective drives the rest of the story?’

cards.label: Rising Complications
cards.placeholder: 2-3 quick obstacles that tighten screws; reveal stakes & character. ‘How does each obstacle sharpen the dilemma?’

cards.label: Twist / Revelation
cards.placeholder: Single beat that flips understanding, raises price, or exposes true antagonist/self, pushing the story towards its climax. ‘What new truth redefines the goal?’

cards.label: Climax
cards.placeholder: Short, decisive confrontation where protagonist’s need meets want; choice under pressure. ‘What irreversible action resolves the central question?’

cards.label: Resolution / Closing Image
cards.placeholder: Echo or invert the opening image; show consequence & thematic aftershock. ‘How has the character/world changed?’

subsections.name: Scene Wizard
subsections.description: Transform your beats into a tight scene structure. The Scene Wizard helps you jump-start your screenplay planning by creating your Scene List based on the inputted story beats.

wizard_config.guidance_default: Construct scenes for a short film. Each scene must do heavy lifting — consolidating multiple story beats into dramatic units. Favor visual storytelling over dialogue. Every scene should have a clear dramatic question that drives it. Each scene should feel complete yet propel into the next.
wizard_config.beat_mapping_strategy: Map the 8 story beats across the scenes. Consolidate related beats into single scenes when appropriate. Ensure the complete arc is covered from opening hook through resolution. Beats should be woven together within scenes, not listed sequentially.

subsections.name: Individual Scenes
subsections.description: Craft each scene with precision. In a short film, every scene must multitask – advance plot, reveal character, and express theme simultaneously. Focus on visual storytelling and minimize dialogue. Each scene should have a clear beginning, middle, and end while contributing to the overall story arc. Populate the goal and climax fields explicitly when inferable.

fields.label: Subtext
fields.placeholder: What are the characters really feeling or wanting beneath their words and actions? The emotional truth they can't or won't say directly.
